conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution volume independent of molecular mass

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: critical conditions were obtained with a ratio 88.8:11.2 (%, v/v) MeOH/water eluent, as indicated by the same elution volume (Ve = 2.33 mL) measured for all PEG chains, independently of their molecular mass
field_evidence.critical_component: The use of critical conditions appropriate to PEO was preferred
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: binary mixture of MeOH/water containing a lithium salt
field_evidence.temperature_celsius: maintained at a constant temperature of 258C
field_evidence.flow_rate: flow rate was set to 1 mL min = 1

conditions.notes: Critical conditions established for PEG standards to be used for PEO block in copolymer analysis.
conditions.physical_address: Universite´s Aix-Marseille I, II & III – CNRS, UMR 6264: Laboratoire Chimie Provence, Spectrome´tries Applique´es a` la Chimie Structurale, F-13397 Marseille Cedex 20, France

conditions.critical_condition_basis: elution volume independent of PEO block size

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: The retention of the SG1-capped PEO-b-PS copolymer was examined at the critical conditions of the PEO block
field_evidence.critical_component: critical conditions of the PEO block
field_evidence.mobile_phase_solvents: 88.8:11.2 (% v/v) MeOH-water mixture containing LiCl (1 mM)

conditions.notes: Analysis of copolymer using critical conditions established for the PEO block. Separation based on PS block size.
conditions.physical_address: Universite´s Aix-Marseille I, II & III – CNRS, UMR 6264: Laboratoire Chimie Provence, Spectrome´tries Applique´es a` la Chimie Structurale, F-13397 Marseille Cedex 20, France
